The pack-level claim is descriptor-verified only (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, flag ai_governance_core_p06_foundation_slice_descriptor_verified); it does NOT make a runtime/enterprise production claim, and that descriptor-verified claim is fully supported by source inspection.

Verified via Read/Grep/Glob only (no Bash, no writes): (1) Plan binding agreement across docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json, docs/closeout-packs/cp00-536/manifest.json, contracts/ai-governance-core-contract.json, and packages/ai-governance/src/registry.js (AI_GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP536_PACK_BINDING) — same risk/unit_count/range/upstream(CP00-535)/next(CP00-537)/next_subphase(RP17.P06.M03.S09). (2) Deliverable distribution {test:2,security_audit:2,implementation:6} and micro-title distribution {Type And Shape Definition:2,Primary Implementation Slice:8} match plan included_units and CP536_REQUIREMENTS. (3) Descriptor-only implementation: all 10 rows (M02: Cross-tenant test, Leak prevention test; M03: Permission matrix row, View/Search/Mutation/Export-download/Share/AI-retrieval decision bindings, Audit hint fields) are present in service.js case set and mirrored in the contract with descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false. (4) freezeCp536Result pins all write/dispatch/leak flags false (writes_product_state, dispatches_*_runtime, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, leak_detected, exposes_*_detail, etc.). (5) Contract<->code consistency is enforced by scripts/validate-rp17-ai-governance-core-contract.mjs, which deep-equals contract p06_foundation_slice_descriptor / cp536_requirements / cp536_no_write_attestation against freshly generated objects and asserts historical_pack_bindings tail == CP00-536; rp17 validate recorded exit 0. (6) Tests in model.test.js cover coverage validity, descriptor-only rows, authority boundary, evidence packets, and handoff; node --test recorded exit 0.

Authority boundary holds: Claude is read-only and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claude_packet.read_only:true), and enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation (enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation:true; claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false). Hermes pattern is used only for a deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptor and a validation object — no runtime receipt or product runtime dispatch (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false). No gate weakening: no production_ready:true or runtime_ready:true anywhere in the pack scope.

CP178+ hardening inputs are valid: readiness auth-preflight is a real, non-empty success (exit 0, stdout 1436 bytes, well-formed result JSON, not 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped); validation-report shows forbidden_tools_present:false and prompt_size_ok; summary-only review was disallowed (security/audit deliverables present) and I performed actual source inspection. Out-of-scope dirty files (scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs, tools/progress-control-room/index.html, workbook/* docs, docs/ldip-integration/.../c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json) are correctly excluded from active_pack_implementation_refs and were not treated as pack content.

Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) referenced by the manifest do not yet exist; this is expected at the read_only_claude_run_raw_capture step of the hardening sequence and is not a defect. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
